description:
You are acting as a platform engineer who is maintaining several Git-based CI/CD pipelines.  
Inside the repository there is already an INI configuration file located at:  

    /home/user/cicd/config/build_settings.ini  

Current contents of that file (including blank lines) are exactly:

[pipeline]
name = core-build
version = 1.4.3-beta
runner = docker

[artifacts]
bucket = ci-build-artifacts
retention_days = 14


Goal  
-----
1. Write **one single shell command** (a single line) that does all of the following in one go:  
   • Parses /home/user/cicd/config/build_settings.ini.  
   • Extracts the value of the key called **version** that is found inside the **[pipeline]** section (value is `1.4.3-beta`).  
   • Creates the directory /home/user/cicd/output if it does not already exist.  
   • Writes the extracted value into a new file located at:  

        /home/user/cicd/output/build_version.log  

     The file must contain **only** the version string followed by a single trailing newline character.  
2. Immediately after the file is written, display the contents of /home/user/cicd/output/build_version.log on the terminal so that anyone running your session can visually confirm the correct value was captured.

Output format requirements (what the automated checker will look for)  
--------------------------------------------------------------------  
• The file /home/user/cicd/output/build_version.log must exist.  
• Its byte-for-byte content must be exactly:  
  `1.4.3-beta` followed by a single Unix newline (`\n`).  
• No other text is allowed in that file.  
• The original INI file must remain unmodified.

Constraints  
-----------  
• Use only **one** compound shell command line (you may use pipes and redirection, but it must be entered as a single line).  
• Do not manually open editors; everything must be done via shell utilities in one line.  
• You do **not** have root privileges; work only under /home/user.  
• After your command runs, use `cat`, `tail`, or a similar tool to show the file’s content on stdout.

When you are done, the file should be in place exactly as described above, and the terminal should have echoed the version string.
truth:
Expected initial environment  
----------------------------  
1. The directory /home/user/cicd/config already exists.  
2. The file /home/user/cicd/config/build_settings.ini already exists and contains **exactly**:

[pipeline]
name = core-build
version = 1.4.3-beta
runner = docker

[artifacts]
bucket = ci-build-artifacts
retention_days = 14


3. The directory /home/user/cicd/output does **not** yet exist.

Verification data after correct execution  
-----------------------------------------  
File that must be created:  
• Path: /home/user/cicd/output/build_version.log  
• Permissions: default user umask (0644 is acceptable).  
• Content (12 bytes total including newline):  
  Line 1: `1.4.3-beta`  
  Line 2: (EOF) — single Unix newline character at the end of line 1.

The automated test will:  
1. Check that /home/user/cicd/output/build_version.log exists.  
2. Run `stat -c "%s" /home/user/cicd/output/build_version.log` and expect the size to be **12** bytes.  
3. Run `cat -A /home/user/cicd/output/build_version.log` and expect the literal output to be `1.4.3-beta$` (the dollar sign shows the newline).
name: task_000000_a739fdfb
